message:
fix(parser): fix HTML entity decoding for numeric entities

Handle both &name; and &#num; formats including hex (&#xHH;) and
decimal (&#NN;). Close bd-xwm3 and bd-wk4r.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-90m7","title":"ER: Implement relationship label rendering and key type highlighting","description":"# ER Diagram Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Relationship labels (the verb phrase between entities) are parsed but not always rendered prominently
- PK/FK/UK key type highlighting in attribute lists: should use bold/color/icon
- Entity attribute comments (quoted strings after attribute) not rendered
- Cardinality symbols at line endpoints need proper crow's foot rendering

## Implementation Plan
1. Ensure relationship labels render centered on the edge path
2. Add visual distinction for key types (PK = key icon or bold, FK = italic, UK = underline)
3. Render attribute comments in lighter/smaller text
4. Verify crow's foot cardinality symbols are correct for all 14 operator variants","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.640543165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:30.983150421Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["er","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":157,"issue_id":"bd-90m7","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)

 {"id":"bd-9cy0","title":"Implement class generics, stereotypes, and annotations","description":"# Class Generics, Stereotypes, and Annotations

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
classDiagram
  class Shape {
    <<interface>>
    +draw()
  }
  class List~T~ {
    +add(T item)
  }
  class Color {
    <<enumeration>>
    RED
    GREEN
    BLUE
  }
  note for Shape "Base interface for all shapes"
\`\`\`
- \`<<interface>>\`, \`<<abstract>>\`, \`<<enumeration>>\`, \`<<service>>\` stereotypes
- \`~T~\` generic type parameters on class names
- \`note for ClassName "text"\` annotations
- \`note "general note"\`

## Current State
- Stereotypes: NOT parsed (<<...>> lines within class blocks are ignored)
- Generics: NOT parsed (tildes treated as part of class name)
- Notes: NOT parsed

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Detect <<stereotype>> lines at top of class block. Parse Class~T~ as class name with generic params. Parse \`note for\` and \`note\` statements.
2. **IR**: Add stereotype: Option<String> and generics: Vec<String> to IrNode for class diagram nodes. Add notes to IR.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Show stereotype in guillemets above class name in header compartment. Show generic params after class name. Notes as yellow sticky-note boxes with connection lines.
4. **Tests**: Interface stereotype, generic class, enumeration, notes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:44.823305009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T17:04:33.747294734Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T17:04:33.746791040Z","close_reason":"Implemented class generics (~T~ syntax parsing), stereotypes (already worked), and three-compartment rendering across all 3 renderers. Parser extracts generic params from class~T~ syntax. IrClassNodeMeta.generics stores them. SVG/terminal/canvas display as ClassName<T> in class header.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["class","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9cy0","depends_on_id":"bd-y2qs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:30.577275181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":150,"issue_id":"bd-9cy0","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-aknv","title":"Parse and render sequence diagram activation/deactivation","description":"# Sequence Activation/Deactivation

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  Alice->>+Bob: Request
  Bob-->>-Alice: Response
  activate Bob
  Bob->>Carol: Forward
  deactivate Bob
\`\`\`
- \`+\` after arrow activates target participant
- \`-\` after arrow deactivates target participant
- \`activate Name\` / \`deactivate Name\` as standalone statements
- Multiple nested activations create stacked boxes
- Activation boxes are narrow rectangles on the lifeline showing active periods

## Current State
- NOT parsed. The +/- modifiers on arrows are ignored.
- No SequenceStatement variant for activate/deactivate.
- Layout does not compute activation box regions.
- SVG renderer does not draw activation boxes.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Add SequenceStatement::Activate(String) and Deactivate(String). Parse \`activate Name\` and \`deactivate Name\` lines. Also parse +/- suffix on arrow operators.
2. **IR**: Add activation tracking. Option: store as a list of (participant_index, start_edge_index, end_edge_index) tuples in a new IrSequenceMeta struct on MermaidDiagramIr.
3. **Layout**: Compute activation box bounds: x = participant center ± half_width, y_start = message y where activated, y_end = message y where deactivated. Handle nested activations with x-offset.
4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw activation boxes as narrow filled rectangles on lifelines.
5. **Tests**: Simple activate/deactivate, nested activations, +/- syntax on arrows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.455081051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:11:18.670511964Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:11:18.669867805Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence activation/deactivation parsing. Supports both standalone 'activate Name'/'deactivate Name' statements and +/- suffix on arrow operators (Alice->>+Bob). Nested activations tracked via per-participant stacks in IrBuilder. Open activations auto-closed at end of diagram. 4 parser tests added. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aknv","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:24.106138908Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":144,"issue_id":"bd-aknv","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":172,"issue_id":"bd-aknv","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL: Also add the missing async message arrow type -) (open arrowhead) to SEQUENCE_OPERATORS. mermaid-js supports: ->>, -->>, ->, -->, -x, --x, -) (async). Currently only 6 of 7 are implemented.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:31:25Z"},{"id":173,"issue_id":"bd-aknv","author":"ubuntu","text":"NOTE: The +/- activation modifiers appear AFTER the arrow operator, e.g. 'Alice->>+Bob'. The parser must detect these suffixed on the target participant name, not on the operator itself. This means modifying the sequence message AST parser at line 3851 to strip trailing +/- from target before node lookup.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:31:25Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-b6gg","title":"Implement accDescription and accTitle accessibility directives","description":"# Accessibility Directives

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
accTitle: Process Flow
accDescr: This diagram shows the order processing pipeline from receipt to delivery.
accDescr {
  Multi-line description
  of the diagram's purpose
}
\`\`\`
- accTitle: sets the accessible title for screen readers (maps to SVG <title> element)
- accDescr: sets the accessible description (maps to SVG <desc> element)
- Both are global, apply to the entire diagram
- Used by all diagram types

## Current State
- accTitle/accDescr are NOT parsed
- SVG renderer already has accessibility support (a11y) but doesn't read these from IR

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Recognize accTitle and accDescr (single-line and multi-line block) directives. Store in MermaidDiagramMeta.
2. **IR**: Add accessible_title: Option<String> and accessible_description: Option<String> to MermaidDiagramMeta.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Emit <title> and <desc> elements within the root <svg> element.
4. **Tests**: Single-line accTitle, accDescr, multi-line accDescr block, SVG output verification.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:12.842946048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.994240852Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates

- cargo check --workspace --all-targets

 {"id":"bd-bfsm","title":"Implement classDef directive parsing and application","description":"# classDef Directive Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
classDef defines reusable CSS-like style classes that can be applied to nodes:
\`\`\`
classDef important fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px
classDef default fill:#fff
class nodeA,nodeB important
\`\`\`

Supported style properties: fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-dasharray, color, font-size, font-weight, font-family, background, border-radius, padding.

## Current State in frankenmermaid
- The \`class nodeA className\` syntax IS parsed (mermaid_parser.rs) and classes are stored on IrNode.classes
- The \`classDef\` syntax itself is NOT parsed — no style definitions are captured
- The SVG renderer does not apply any class-based styling to nodes

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser** (mermaid_parser.rs): Parse \`classDef className prop:value,prop:value\` lines. Store in a new \`IrStyleDef\` type in MermaidDiagramIr (add to fm-core).
2. **IR** (fm-core/src/lib.rs): Add \`style_defs: BTreeMap<String, IrStyleDef>\` to MermaidDiagramIr. IrStyleDef contains fill, stroke, stroke_width, stroke_dasharray, color, etc.
3. **SVG Renderer** (fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs): When rendering a node, look up its classes in style_defs and apply fill/stroke/etc. as inline SVG attributes.
4. **Terminal Renderer**: No visual impact (terminal doesn't support colors in basic mode).

## Test Plan
- Parse classDef with single property
- Parse classDef with multiple comma-separated properties
- Apply class to node and verify SVG attributes
- Verify default classDef applies to unclassed nodes
- Verify classDef with quoted values","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.353430251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23.643658982Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","depends_on_id":"bd-77gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:22.387976601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

The parser implementation should handle classDef lines in the generic pre-processing phase (before diagram-type-specific parsing) so it works across all types. The IR's style_defs field is on MermaidDiagramIr which is shared by all diagram types.","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bium","title":"Implement flowchart parallel edge syntax (A & B --> C & D)","description":"# Flowchart Parallel Edge Syntax

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
flowchart LR
  A & B --> C & D
\`\`\`
This creates 4 edges: A→C, A→D, B→C, B→D. The & operator allows declaring multiple source/target nodes in a single edge statement.

## Current State
- NOT parsed. The & character is not recognized in edge declarations.
- Mermaid parser's edge AST only supports single source → single target.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: In parse_edge_statement_asts(), before looking for arrow operators, split node references on '&'. If either side contains '&', expand to multiple FlowAst::Edge entries (cartesian product of sources × targets).
2. **Tests**: A & B --> C (2 edges), A --> B & C (2 edges), A & B --> C & D (4 edges), single node (no &, unchanged behavior).

## Considerations
- The & operator is only valid in flowchart, not other diagram types
- Must handle whitespace: 'A&B' vs 'A & B' vs 'A &B'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:14.744584229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T01:31:57.010578087Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T01:31:57.010075205Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser"],"comments":[{"id":195,"issue_id":"bd-bium","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

-{"id":"bd-xwm3","title":"Implement subgraph direction override","description":"# Subgraph Direction Override

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
flowchart LR
  subgraph sub1
    direction TB
    A --> B
  end
  sub1 --> C
\`\`\`
Each subgraph can have its own direction that overrides the parent graph direction. This affects layout within that subgraph only.

## Current State
- The \`direction\` keyword IS parsed within subgraphs
- The direction is stored on IrSubgraph
- The layout engine does NOT honor per-subgraph direction — it uses a single global direction

## Implementation Plan
1. **Layout** (fm-layout/src/lib.rs): In the Sugiyama layout, when processing nodes within a subgraph/cluster, check if the cluster has a direction override. If so, apply rank assignment and coordinate assignment with that direction for nodes within the cluster.
2. **Tests**: Create a flowchart with mixed directions (parent LR, child TB). Verify child nodes are laid out vertically within the horizontal parent flow.

## Complexity
This is a non-trivial layout change. The current Sugiyama implementation uses a single global direction. Supporting per-subgraph direction requires partitioning the layout into independent sub-layouts and stitching them together. Consider a phased approach: first support the feature in the IR, then implement the layout logic.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.742818041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T01:53:43.599388584Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","layout","parity","parser"],"comments":[{"id":142,"issue_id":"bd-xwm3","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

+{"id":"bd-xwm3","title":"Implement subgraph direction override","description":"# Subgraph Direction Override

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
flowchart LR
  subgraph sub1
    direction TB
    A --> B
  end
  sub1 --> C
\`\`\`
Each subgraph can have its own direction that overrides the parent graph direction. This affects layout within that subgraph only.

## Current State
- The \`direction\` keyword IS parsed within subgraphs
- The direction is stored on IrSubgraph
- The layout engine does NOT honor per-subgraph direction — it uses a single global direction

## Implementation Plan
1. **Layout** (fm-layout/src/lib.rs): In the Sugiyama layout, when processing nodes within a subgraph/cluster, check if the cluster has a direction override. If so, apply rank assignment and coordinate assignment with that direction for nodes within the cluster.
2. **Tests**: Create a flowchart with mixed directions (parent LR, child TB). Verify child nodes are laid out vertically within the horizontal parent flow.

## Complexity
This is a non-trivial layout change. The current Sugiyama implementation uses a single global direction. Supporting per-subgraph direction requires partitioning the layout into independent sub-layouts and stitching them together. Consider a phased approach: first support the feature in the IR, then implement the layout logic.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.742818041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T04:16:31.599729614Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T04:16:31.599220411Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","layout","parity","parser"],"comments":[{"id":142,"issue_id":"bd-xwm3","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-y2qs","title":"Implement class member visibility modifiers and type annotations","description":"# Class Member Visibility and Type Annotations

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
classDiagram
  class Animal {
    +String name
    -int age
    #List~Food~ diet
    ~void breathe()
    +eat(Food food) bool
    +sleep()* : void
  }
\`\`\`
- \`+\` public, \`-\` private, \`#\` protected, \`~\` package
- Type before name for attributes, after parentheses for return types
- \`*\` suffix for abstract methods
- \`$\` suffix for static methods

## Current State
- Class blocks ARE parsed, member lines are captured
- Visibility modifiers are NOT extracted — stored as raw text in member string
- Type annotations are NOT parsed separately from names
- No rendering of visibility symbols

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: When parsing class block members, extract leading +/-/#/~ as visibility. Parse \`type name\` for attributes, \`name(params) returnType\` for methods. Detect */$ suffixes.
2. **IR**: Add IrClassMember { visibility: Visibility, name: String, return_type: Option<String>, parameters: Vec<String>, is_abstract: bool, is_static: bool }.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Render three-compartment class boxes: header (class name + stereotype), attributes section, methods section. Prepend visibility symbol. Show types.
4. **Tests**: All visibility markers, attribute types, method signatures, abstract/static.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:44.660117013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:33:44.181153568Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T09:33:44.180655345Z","close_reason":"Implemented class member visibility + three-compartment rendering. Parser already extracts +/-/#/~ visibility, types, static/abstract. Terminal renderer shows UML class boxes with compartment separators. SVG renderer shows three-compartment boxes with stereotypes, visibility symbols, type annotations, and separator lines.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["class","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":149,"issue_id":"bd-y2qs","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

 {"id":"bd-y6o8","title":"Implement style and linkStyle directive parsing and rendering","description":"# style / linkStyle Directive Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
Inline styling for specific nodes and edges:
\`\`\`
style nodeId fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px
linkStyle 0 stroke:#ff3,stroke-width:4px,color:red
linkStyle default stroke:#333
\`\`\`

style applies to a node by ID. linkStyle applies to an edge by index (0-based declaration order) or 'default' for all edges.

## Current State
- Parser recognizes style/linkStyle lines but does not capture the property map
- No rendering path for inline styles

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`style nodeId prop:value,...\` and store as IrNodeStyle on the IrNode. Parse \`linkStyle index prop:value,...\` and store as IrEdgeStyle on the IrEdge.
2. **IR**: Add \`inline_style: Option<IrInlineStyle>\` to IrNode and IrEdge. IrInlineStyle is a BTreeMap<String, String>.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Apply inline_style as SVG attributes (fill → rect fill, stroke → rect stroke, etc.). For edges, apply to <path> elements.
4. **Tests**: Verify style is applied in SVG output, verify linkStyle by index, verify default linkStyle.

## Considerations
- Property names must be mapped from CSS-like (stroke-width) to SVG attributes
- Sanitize values to prevent XSS in SVG output","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.486402415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23.800287285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","depends_on_id":"bd-77gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:23.246993348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":140,"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

The parser must split on commas and apply the style to each node.","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-yh4v","title":"Implement gantt date parsing, task types, and duration computation","description":"# Gantt Date/Duration/Task Type Parsing

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
gantt
  dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
  title Project Plan
  excludes weekends
  section Phase 1
  Design           :done, des1, 2024-01-01, 30d
  Implementation   :active, impl1, after des1, 60d
  Testing          :crit, test1, after impl1, 2024-05-01
  Milestone        :milestone, m1, after test1, 0d
\`\`\`
- dateFormat: YYYY-MM-DD, DD/MM/YYYY, etc.
- Task types: done, active, crit (critical), milestone
- Duration: Nd (days), after taskId (dependency), absolute dates
- excludes: weekends, specific dates

## Current State
- Parser recognizes sections and task lines, but does NOT extract dates, types, durations, or dependencies
- Layout has a gantt algorithm but operates on generic nodes, not time-aware bars

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: After the colon in task lines, parse comma-separated metadata: type (done/active/crit/milestone), id, start (date or 'after id'), end (date or duration). Parse dateFormat directive. Parse excludes.
2. **IR**: Add IrGanttMeta { date_format, excludes, sections: Vec<IrGanttSection> } with IrGanttTask { id, name, task_type, start, end, depends_on }.
3. **Layout**: Compute actual x-positions from dates. Apply excludes to skip weekends. Position milestone as diamonds.
4. **Tests**: Date formats, durations, after-dependencies, excludes, milestones, crit tasks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.294100500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31.708821785Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gantt","parity","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","depends_on_id":"bd-0bu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:28.205838996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":155,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"},{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL GANTT DIRECTIVES TO PARSE:
- todayMarker stroke-width:5px,stroke:#0f0,opacity:0.5 → custom styling for the today line. Store as optional style string in IrGanttMeta.
- tickInterval 1day / 1week / 1month → controls axis label granularity. Store as enum (Day/Week/Month/Quarter/Year) in IrGanttMeta.
- inclusiveEndDates → when true, task end dates include the end day. Store as bool in IrGanttMeta.
- weekday monday → sets start of week. Store as optional u8 in IrGanttMeta.
- axisFormat %Y-%m-%d → date format for axis labels. Store as String in IrGanttMeta.

These must ALL be parsed for full parity. Update the IrGanttMeta type definition in bd-0bu2 accordingly.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31Z"}]}
